author: rig-relay-executor
title: SQLite-to-Postgres Data Migration Procedure
summary: Complete procedure for migrating from the existing SQLite database (opencode.db) to a Postgres or PGlite destination. Covers pre-requisites, FK audit, data copy, post-migration verification, and rollback.

prerequisites.description: Pre-requisites that must be satisfied before running the migration command.

steps.detail: Postgres: ensure pg is running and accessible via connection string. PGlite: bundled with app — no separate server needed.
The destination Postgres schema must already exist with all required tables. Run `opencode db migrate-pg-schema` (or equivalent drizzle-kit migrate) before data migration.
The SQLite database at Database.getPath() must exist and be readable. Run `opencode db path` to verify the path.
Provide the Postgres connection string as a positional argument or set the PG_URL environment variable. The connection string format: postgresql://user:pass@host:port/dbname

pre_migration_rollback.description: Before any data is copied, the migration command renames the active SQLite database file to preserve a rollback point.

steps.detail: The active SQLite file (full path from Database.getPath()) is renamed atomically. If a .pre-pg-migration file already exists, the migration aborts to prevent accidental overwrite.

steps.action: Open renamed file as read-only source
steps.detail: The renamed file becomes the data source. The original path is now free — a fresh empty SQLite database will be created on next app launch if OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL is not set.

fk_audit.description: Foreign key integrity audit performed before data copy. Can be skipped with --force.
fk_audit.audit_sql: For each FK: SELECT <fk.column> AS val FROM <fk.table> WHERE <where clause> LIMIT 1000
fk_audit.on_violation: Migration halts with a detailed violation report. Use --force to skip audit and migrate anyway.

data_copy.description: Data is copied table-by-table from SQLite source to Postgres destination in parent-before-child order.

type_conversions.json_columns: Columns stored as text({mode:'json'}) in SQLite are parsed via JSON.parse() before insertion as jsonb() in Postgres.
type_conversions.boolean_columns: Columns stored as integer({mode:'boolean'}) in SQLite are converted to boolean (1/true/'1' → true) for Postgres boolean columns.

data_copy.insert_strategy: INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING per row, batched through a single client connection.
data_copy.verification: Row count reported per table during copy.

post_migration_verification.description: After data copy completes, every table is verified by row count and SHA256 checksum. Can be skipped with --skip-verify.

checks.detail: SELECT COUNT(*) is executed on both SQLite source and Postgres destination for every table. Counts must match exactly.
checks.on_failure: Migration fails with discrepancy report. Rollback backup preserved.

checks.detail: All rows are read in deterministic order (ORDER BY 1) from both SQLite and Postgres. Each row is canonicalized to tab-separated values (null → \N), newline-delimited, then SHA256-hashed. Checksums must match exactly.
checks.on_failure: Migration fails with discrepancy report showing both checksums. Rollback backup preserved.

post_migration_verification.output: Per-table status (OK or MISMATCH with row counts and truncated SHA256). Summary passes or fails.

completion.description: Steps performed after successful verification to mark migration as complete.

steps.detail: A flag file at <dbDir>/.rig/postgres-migration-complete is created. The flag file content is the ISO 8601 timestamp of completion.
Set the OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL environment variable to the Postgres connection string. The application will use Postgres instead of SQLite on next launch.

rollback.description: Procedure to revert to the SQLite database after migration.

steps.detail: Remove or unset the OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL environment variable so the application falls back to SQLite.
Rename the .pre-pg-migration backup back to the original database filename.
If the migration flag exists, remove it to allow re-running migration.
Launch the application and verify that data is accessible from the restored SQLite database.
steps.verification: opencode db path && opencode db 'SELECT count(*) FROM session'

rollback.data_safety: The original SQLite database is preserved intact under the .pre-pg-migration suffix throughout the entire migration. Verification failure does NOT delete the backup — it remains for manual restore.
rollback.re_migration: To re-run migration after rollback: remove the .pre-pg-migration backup (or restore it first), ensure the destination Postgres tables are empty, and run the command again.

flags.--force: Skip FK audit and proceed with migration even if orphaned records exist.
flags.--dry-run: Run FK audit only. Does not rename SQLite, connect to Postgres, or copy data.
flags.--skip-verify: Skip post-migration row count and SHA256 checksum verification. Intended for CI/testing where verification is handled externally.

rollback_backup_exists.condition: .pre-pg-migration file already exists from a previous migration attempt.
rollback_backup_exists.message: Rollback backup already exists. Remove it manually or restore it first to re-run migration.

postgres_connection_failure.condition: Cannot connect to the specified Postgres URL.
postgres_connection_failure.message: Postgres connection failed or URL is invalid.
postgres_connection_failure.resolution: Verify the connection string, ensure the Postgres server is running, and check firewall/SSL settings.

verification_mismatch.condition: Row count or checksum mismatch detected after data copy.
verification_mismatch.message: Verification FAILED — discrepancies found. Rollback backup preserved at <backupPath>.
verification_mismatch.resolution: Investigate the cause (data corruption, schema mismatch, type conversion error), fix the issue, drop Postgres tables, clear the backup, and re-run migration.
